title: Maresia exacoides

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Maresia exacoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Maresia exacoides are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and support overall well-being.

This family is part of the order Proteales, a group of flowering plants known for their unique flowers and seeds.
